Functions for initializing, locking, enabling, and disabling peripheral configurations.

# Purpose
This C header file declares a set of functions for managing peripheral configurations in an embedded system. The functions include [`periph_config_init`](<#periph_config_init>), which initializes the peripheral configuration system, and [`periph_config_acquire_lock`](<#periph_config_acquire_lock>) and [`periph_config_release_lock`](<#periph_config_release_lock>), which manage access control to ensure safe concurrent operations. The functions [`periph_config_enable`](<#periph_config_enable>) and [`periph_config_disable`](<#periph_config_disable>) are used to enable or disable a specific peripheral, identified by a pointer `periph` and a `rcc_bit` that likely represents a control bit in a register. The file includes the standard library `<stdint.h>` to use fixed-width integer types, ensuring portability across different platforms.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stdint.h`
